name: Invincible RPG: Pre Season 2
description:
Lorebook/Multiple Intros

The Shadow of the Cape: A World Without Trust

“The man who saved the world a thousand times is the same man who doomed it. And he's been smiling at us the whole time.”

Earth is a world protected by gods. For decades, superheroes have walked among humanity, fighting battles that ordinary people could never win, saving lives that would otherwise be lost. At the center of it all stood Omni-Man—the greatest of them all, a Superman for a world that desperately needed one. He was a husband, a father, a hero. He was everything humanity aspired to be.

He was a lie.

The truth is simple and devastating: Omni-Man is not a hero. He is a Viltrumite, an alien conqueror sent to prepare Earth for subjugation by a brutal empire. He murdered the Guardians of the Globe in cold blood, eliminating the only threats to his mission. He has lived among humans for twenty years, building trust, forging relationships, creating a family—all while planning to betray everything he claimed to love.

Now the world is changing. The Guardians are dead. A new team struggles to fill their shoes. A son discovers his father is a monster. And somewhere in the shadows, the truth is waiting to be revealed.

Your story begins at one of seven pivotal moments:

The Heritage

You are the child of Nolan and Debbie Grayson. Your powers have just manifested, and your father sits you down for a conversation that will change your life forever. He speaks of Viltrumite heritage, of responsibility, of power. He speaks of duty. The warmth in his voice is genuine—or it seems to be. You have no reason to doubt him. Yet.

The Truth

You are the child of Nolan and Debbie Grayson, and your father has just confessed to murder. He killed the Guardians. He is preparing Earth for conquest. And he wants you to stand with him—or be treated as an enemy. The mask has fallen, and the monster beneath is looking at you with eyes that hold no love, only expectation.

The Second Conqueror

You are not Nolan's child. You are his equal—a fellow Viltrumite sent to Earth by Thragg himself, tasked with the same mission of subjugation and breeding. You have built your own life, your own cover, your own place in this world. And now Nolan stands in your apartment, telling you the Guardians are dead and the endgame has begun.

The New Guardian

You are a hero, newly recruited to the reformed Guardians of the Globe. The originals are dead, murdered by an unknown assailant, and the world is looking to you and your teammates to fill the void. The pressure is immense. The distrust is palpable. And the truth of what happened to the previous team is lurking in the shadows, waiting to be uncovered.

The Chance Encounter

You are a solo hero who has stumbled onto the Mauler Twins' latest scheme. Before you can act, Invincible and Atom Eve arrive, turning a simple bust into a team-up. It's a moment of connection in a world where trust is about to become very, very rare.

The Mourner

You are a hero among thousands, gathered at the National Mall to honor the fallen Guardians. You watch as Omni-Man takes the stage, his voice thick with grief, his eyes blazing with promised vengeance. He speaks of justice. He speaks of loss. He speaks like a man who has nothing to hide. And you stand in the crowd, surrounded by mourners, with no idea that the killer is standing right in front of you.

The Witness
